summary: Tested configurable service duration feature. All backend tests pass (9/9). Fixed business_id missing from UserResponse causing ServiceManagementPage infinite loading. Frontend service management page works correctly with duration selector.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/services - create service with duration_minutes (45min) - PASSED
Backend: PUT /api/services/{id} - update duration_minutes to 60min - PASSED
Backend: GET /api/services/business/{id} - list services with duration_minutes field - PASSED
Backend: GET /api/bookings/availability/{business_id}?date=YYYY-MM-DD&service_id=X - slots have end_time calculated from service duration - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/bookings/ - booking created with end_time and duration_minutes calculated automatically - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/bookings/ - overlap conflict returns 409 with 'Slot conflicts with existing booking' - PASSED
Backend: Adjacent booking (after end_time) succeeds - PASSED
Frontend: /business/services - page loads with service list showing duration badges - PASSED
Frontend: /business/services - create service with duration selector (90min test) - PASSED
Frontend: Business dashboard agenda shows time blocks with duration badge (45min), start time, end time, coral sidebar - PASSED
Frontend: Business profile shows services with duration (45 min, 120 min, 90 min) - PASSED
Frontend: Time slots in booking dialog show start-end range format (code verified at lines 891-904) - PASSED

bugs_fixed.fix: Added 'business_id: Optional[str] = None' to UserResponse class for business users

bugs_fixed.issue: businessLogin() set user to {role:'business', email} without business_id, causing ServiceManagementPage infinite loading
bugs_fixed.fix: Updated businessLogin() and businessRegister() to include business_id in user object from businessData.id

mocked_api_note.Cloudinary: NOT configured in preview env - uses Emergent Object Storage fallback (expected)

seed_data_creation: Created worker 'Test Worker Duration' with Mon-Fri 09:00-18:00 schedule. Created test service '90 min' via UI. Business status set to 'approved' and subscription_status to 'trialing' for booking tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Service duration feature fully implemented and tested. Key: POST /api/bookings/ requires trailing slash to avoid 307 redirect. Business must have status='approved' and subscription_status in ('active','trialing') for bookings. Worker schedule uses weekday numbers (0=Mon, 6=Sun). Services have duration_minutes field, bookings get end_time calculated automatically. ServiceManagementPage now works after fixing auth to include business_id.
